DISCOVERY / LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 301, AS TO THE FEB-14-2025 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MURATA ELECTRONICS NORTH AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. OFF CALENDAR. THE GALEK DECLARATION SHOWS THAT WHILE MOVING PARTY'S COUNSEL ATTEMPTED TO MEET AND CONFER WITH THE PLEADING PARTY, PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL DID NOT COMPLY WITH THE STATUTORY REQUIREMENT TO MEET AND CONFER. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IS ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH THE CODE AND MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON, BY TELEPHONE, OR BY VIDEO CONFERENCE, AS REQUIRED BY CCP 435.5(A)(1). (SEE ID. ["AS PART OF THE MEET AND CONFER PROCESS, . . . [T]HE PARTY WHO FILED THE PLEADING SHALL PROVIDE LEGAL SUPPORT FOR ITS POSITION THAT THE PLEADING IS LEGALLY SUFFICIENT, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, HOW THE PLEADING COULD BE AMENDED TO CURE ANY LEGAL INSUFFICIENCY."].) THE RESPONSE TO THE COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY MARCH 21, 2025. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT MURATA ELECTRONICS NORTH AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT GRANTS THIS ALTERNATIVE RELIEF. PLAINTIFFS SHALL FILE WITHIN 10 COURT DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. THE MOTION TO STRIKE THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE AND EXEMPLARY DAMAGES FROM THE SOON-TO-BE-SUPERSEDED FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED AS MOOT. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/CVA)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MURATA ELECTRONICS NORTH AMERICA, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT.

In this pleadings motion, all parties who have been served and have appeared in the case request that the court grant leave for plaintiffs to file a Second Amended Complaint as stipulated to by appearing parties pursuant to the Galek Declaration, paragraph 14, and the Cohen Declaration, paragraph 13. The court grants this alternative relief. Plaintiffs shall file within 10 court days of entry of this order. The motion to strike the prayer for punitive and exemplary damages from the soon-to-be-superseded First Amended Complaint is denied as moot.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-05-2025 Continued To Jun-04-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-14-2025 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT MURATA ELECTRONICS NORTH AMERICA, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Off calendar. The Galek declaration shows that while moving party's counsel attempted to meet and confer with the pleading party, Plaintiff's counsel did not comply with the statutory requirement to meet and confer. Plaintiff's counsel is ordered to comply with the Code and meet and confer in person, by telephone, or by video conference, as required by CCP 435.5(a)(1). (See id. ["As part of the meet and confer process, . . . [t]he party who filed the pleading shall provide legal support for its position that the pleading is legally sufficient, or, in the alternative, how the pleading could be amended to cure any legal insufficiency."].) The response to the complaint is now due by March 21, 2025.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
